skill.name: using-neon
skill.description: Guides and best practices for working with Neon Serverless Postgres. Covers getting started, local development with Neon, choosing a connection method, Neon features, authentication (@neondatabase/...
skill.summary: Guide for working with Neon Serverless Postgres including connection methods, features, and best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 29 static findings are false positives. This is a documentation skill that provides references to Neon Serverless Postgres documentation. The scanner misinterpreted example curl commands in markdown code blocks as shell execution, hardcoded URLs as potential exfiltration, and metadata text as cryptographic algorithms. No actual security risks present.

medium_findings.title: External Commands False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple lines, but this skill contains only documentation with example curl commands in markdown code blocks. No actual code execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines contain only markdown documentation text and example curl commands in code blocks, not actual executable shell commands

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged URLs at lines 4, 34, 37 as potential data exfiltration. These are legitimate references to official Neon documentation URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official Neon documentation (neon.com, github.com/neondatabase) - legitimate documentation references, not exfiltration

medium_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged lines 3 and 46 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is metadata and markdown headings, not cryptographic code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter description field, line 46 is markdown heading - no cryptographic code exists in this file

content.value_statement: Neon is a serverless Postgres platform that separates compute and storage. This skill provides guides and best practices for connecting, managing, and scaling Neon databases in your applications.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through Neon Serverless Postgres setup and configuration
Help choose the right connection method for different platforms and runtimes
Explain Neon features like branching, autoscaling, and scale-to-zero
Provide references to official Neon documentation for verification
Assist with Neon authentication and API integration
Support developer tools like Neon CLI, VSCode extension, and MCP server
limitations: Does not execute database queries or manage actual Neon projects
Does not provide live troubleshooting for Neon service outages
Cannot access user-specific Neon account information or project data
Does not generate code that creates security vulnerabilities

use_cases.title: New Developer Setting Up Database
use_cases.description: A developer new to Neon wants to set up their first serverless Postgres database. The skill guides them through creating a project, getting connection strings, and choosing the right driver.
use_cases.target_user: Developers new to Neon Serverless Postgres

use_cases.title: Choosing Connection Method
use_cases.description: A developer building a serverless application needs help selecting between the Neon serverless driver, traditional Postgres drivers, or ORM integrations based on their platform.

use_cases.title: Using Advanced Features
use_cases.description: A team wants to leverage Neon features like database branching for development workflows or autoscaling for production. The skill provides context and documentation links.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams using advanced Neon features

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Neon
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to set up a Neon Serverless Postgres database for my project. What are the first steps?
prompt_templates.scenario: New user wants to understand basic setup process

prompt_templates.title: Connect to Neon from Application
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I connect my [language/framework] application to Neon Serverless Postgres?
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs connection guidance for specific technology

prompt_templates.title: Choose Connection Method
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the best way to connect to Neon from a serverless function?
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer building serverless application needs guidance

prompt_templates.title: Use Database Branching
prompt_templates.prompt: How does Neon database branching work and when should I use it?
prompt_templates.scenario: Team wants to leverage branching for development workflows

output_examples.input: How do I connect my Node.js application to Neon?
output_examples.output:
For Node.js applications, you have several options:

1. **Neon Serverless Driver** (recommended for serverless):
   ```bash
   npm install @neondatabase/serverless
   ```
   Uses HTTP/WebSocket for serverless environments.

2. **Traditional pg driver**:
   ```bash
   npm install pg
   ```
   Use for long-running processes.

3. **Drizzle ORM**:
   ```bash
   npm install drizzle-orm @neondatabase/serverless
   ```
   Type-safe ORM integration.

Connection string format: `postgresql://user:password@ep-xxx.us-east-1.aws.neon.tech/dbname?sslmode=require`

Neon Serverless Postgres offers several key features:

1. **Serverless Architecture** - Separate compute and storage with automatic scale-to-zero
2. **Database Branching** - Create instant clones for development and testing
3. **Instant Restore** - Point-in-time recovery with instant restores
4. **Autoscaling** - Automatic compute scaling based on demand
5. **Full Postgres Compatibility** - Works with any Postgres client, ORM, or tool

The documentation at https://neon.com/docs has detailed guides for each feature.

best_practices: Always use the Neon serverless driver for serverless functions and edge runtimes to avoid connection limits
Reference official Neon documentation for the most up-to-date connection strings and API information
Use environment variables for connection strings rather than hardcoding credentials
anti_patterns: Do not assume Neon's connection pooling behavior matches traditional Postgres - it uses HTTP/WebSocket in serverless
Do not guess documentation URLs - use the llms.txt index to find correct pages
Do not make claims about Neon pricing or limits without checking current documentation

faq.question: What is Neon Serverless Postgres?
faq.answer: Neon is a serverless Postgres platform that separates compute and storage. It offers autoscaling, database branching, instant restore, and scale-to-zero capabilities while remaining fully compatible with Postgres.

faq.question: How do I connect to Neon from my application?
faq.answer: Use the @neondatabase/serverless driver for serverless environments, or traditional Postgres drivers like pg for long-running processes. The skill can help you choose the right method for your platform.

faq.question: What is database branching in Neon?
faq.answer: Database branching creates an instant clone of your database. This is useful for development workflows, testing, and CI/CD pipelines where you need isolated database copies.

faq.question: Does Neon work with my ORM?
faq.answer: Yes, Neon works with any ORM that supports Postgres, including Drizzle ORM, Prisma, Sequelize, and SQLAlchemy. The skill includes specific guidance for Drizzle integration.

faq.question: Can I use Neon with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed to help Claude Code assist with Neon Serverless Postgres setup and management. It provides guidance for database operations within Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What are Neon's scaling capabilities?
faq.answer: Neon offers automatic scale-to-zero (compute suspends when idle) and autoscaling (compute scales based on workload). You can configure these settings in your Neon project dashboard.
